scope: Pass B full-surface expansion. Full documented Sharetribe Integration API surface (https://www.sharetribe.com/api-reference/integration.html, fetched and parsed 2026-07-03) accounted for below, covered_by a stream/write or excluded with a real reason. Adds 3 new read streams (marketplace, files, file_attachments) alongside the 4 legacy-parity streams (listings, users, transactions, events), and 13 new write actions covering the listing lifecycle (create/update/close/open/approve), user lifecycle (approve/update_profile/update_permissions/verify_email), transaction transitions (transition/transition_speculative/update_metadata), availability exceptions (create/delete), and stock (compare_and_set/stock_adjustments create). capabilities.write flips to true. Endpoints whose ONLY query shape requires a mandatory per-parent-resource filter (listingId/transactionId with no unfiltered-list alternative) are excluded as out_of_scope rather than forced into an awkward single-parent-scoped stream; endpoints reachable only by single-id GET with no corresponding list/query endpoint are excluded as duplicate_of (or out_of_scope when no covering stream exists at all); the multipart-upload images endpoint is excluded as binary_payload.

excluded.reason: single-listing detail fetch for a resource the listings stream (GET /integration_api/listings/query with no filter body, listing every listing) already lists in full
single-user detail fetch for a resource the users stream already lists in full
single-transaction detail fetch for a resource the transactions stream already lists in full

every documented query requires either transactionId or ids (the docs state explicitly: 'Either transactionId or ids must be provided') — there is no unfiltered list-all-messages shape, and no natural connector-instance-level config value (a single fixed transaction id) that would make this a meaningful sync-all stream; a caller wanting a specific transaction's messages should read the transactions stream and cross-reference, or this could be added as a fan_out stream keyed off transaction ids in a future capability expansion

the only query shape is scoped to a single mandatory listingId (plus a mandatory start/end range capped at 366 days) — there is no unfiltered list-all-availability-exceptions endpoint across the whole marketplace, so this cannot back a top-level sync stream the way listings/users/transactions/events can
the only query shape is scoped to a single mandatory listingId, same reasoning as availability_exceptions/query — no unfiltered list-all-stock-adjustments endpoint exists

single-stock-reservation detail fetch by mandatory id, with NO corresponding list/query endpoint at all (stock reservations are reachable only via the stockReservation relationship of a stock adjustment) — there is no way to enumerate reservation ids to iterate this endpoint over

accepts a multipart/form-data file upload body, not a JSON payload; the engine's declarative HTTP write dialect has no multipart-body primitive. Listing/user image attachment (imageId/profileImageId) already flows through create_listing/update_listing/update_user_profile for an id previously uploaded out-of-band
